questions.prompt:
Reading — Read: "Nour has two cats and one dog. The cats are black. The dog is brown."
Which sentence is TRUE?
questions.explanation: Two cats + one dog = three pets, so (c) is true. The others contradict the text: Nour has one dog, not two (a); the dog is brown, not black (b); and the cats are black, not brown (d). Combine the facts before choosing.

questions.prompt: Grammar — Choose the fully correct sentence.

options.text: She watch a film every weekend.
She watches a film every weekend.
She watches a film every weekends.

questions.explanation: With she, the present simple adds -es: she watches. We also say every weekend (singular after every), so (b) is fully correct. (a) and (d) drop the third-person -s, and (c) wrongly pluralizes after every — every is always followed by a singular noun.
The error is (a): man has an irregular plural, men, never "mans". The others are also irregular and correct: foot → feet, tooth → teeth, mouse → mice. None of these common nouns takes a regular -s.

questions.prompt: Vocabulary — Choose the word that best completes the sentence: "This bag is cheap, but that phone is very ___."
questions.explanation: "But" signals a contrast with cheap, so the missing word is its opposite, expensive: cheap ↔ expensive. Small, fast and happy are real adjectives, but none is the opposite of cheap, so they break the contrast the sentence sets up.

questions.prompt: Grammar — Put the words in the correct order: (study / what / does / he)
questions.explanation: An ordinary-verb question is: question word + does + subject + base verb → What does he study? "What he does study" misplaces the subject, "studies" double-marks the -s (it lives on does, not the main verb), and "What studies he?" drops the helper does and keeps statement order.

questions.prompt: Spelling — Choose the correctly spelled plural of "shelf".
questions.explanation: Some nouns ending in -f or -fe change f to v and add -es: shelf → shelves (like wolf → wolves, half → halves). "Shelfs" and "shelfes" keep the f instead of switching to v, and "shelvs" changes to v but drops the needed -e before -s.
